evidence_points: In low-risk women, unscheduled bleeding within 6 months of starting HRT (or within 3 months of dose/preparation change) can be managed with HRT/progestogen adjustments for a total of 6 months.
If bleeding first presents after 6 months from HRT initiation, or persists >3 months after regimen change, offer urgent transvaginal ultrasound (within 6 weeks).
Immediate urgent TVUS is advised when bleeding is heavy/prolonged or there are risk factors for endometrial cancer.
On TVUS, fully visualised uniform endometrium of <=4 mm on continuous combined HRT or <=7 mm on sequential HRT is generally low risk; continue adjustment pathway with reassessment.
Thicker endometrium than these thresholds should prompt urgent endometrial assessment on a cancer-suspicion pathway.

Ensure adequate monthly progestogen exposure in women with a uterus; sequential regimens should include minimum progestogen days per cycle and transition plan to continuous combined regimen at appropriate age/duration.
summary: Urgent TVUS threshold: delayed-onset bleeding, persistent bleeding after change, heavy/prolonged bleeding, or risk factors
Endometrial thresholds differ by regimen (<=4 mm ccHRT, <=7 mm sHRT)
Escalate thickened endometrium or concerning findings to urgent cancer pathway
